summary: Split from #2345 to land item-only 100% matches. The PR replaced 10 item-function stubs with matching C implementations across Pokemon/item projectiles, shell behavior, Ness yoyo, Kirby cutter beam, and related item helpers, while also tightening item var/attribute structs and several placeholder prototypes. decomp.dev reported +2976 matched code bytes, +40 matched data bytes, and 13 total new matches including .sdata/.sdata2 items.

key_files.role: Matched Staryu/Hitodeman motion-1 animation tick and exposed an attribute float previously hidden by padding.
Matched Kabigon helper that changes motion state, resets Y velocity, sets scale, installs hitlag callbacks, and plays sfx 0x2724.
Matched scale-up tick using external double constants and HSD_JObjGetScale/HSD_JObjSetScale.
Matched cutter beam physics using facing_dir, speed, angle, cosf/sinf, speed decay, lifetime child visibility, and PAD_STACK.
Matched charged shadow ball animation scaling and hitbox scale update.
Matched yoyo returning-state physics using matrix setup/concat, link hit detection, zero velocity, and fighter yoyo hitbox position.
Matched Octorok/Oldottosea launch helper, including velocity reset, collision facing, optional freeze item spawn, and motion selection.
Matched Patapata spawn helper that creates item kind 0xD4, sets facing/collision facing, initializes motion, and installs jumped_on callback.
Matched Red Shell motion-5 animation timer logic and added a temporary f32 in effect spawn helper for codegen.
Matched green/z shell slow-down physics, cleanup calls, zeroing, and transition back to grounded/bouncing behavior.
Replaced broad padding with typed offset fields needed by matched item code.
Split Climbers Ice attribute padding into typed f32 fields at +14, +18, +1C, +24.
Converted yoyo helper declarations from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed ItemLink/Vec3/itYoyoAttributes/Item signatures.

what_changed: Replaced stubs for 10 listed 100% item matches: itHitodeman_UnkMotion1_Anim, it_802CA074, it_802CAE94, itKirbycutterbeam_UnkMotion0_Phys, itMewtwoshadowball_UnkMotion17_Anim, itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Phys, it_802E27B4, it_802E16F8, itRshell_UnkMotion5_Anim, and itZrshell_UnkMotion6_Phys.

Expanded item var and attribute layouts in itCommonItems.h and itCharItems.h, including Mewtwo Shadow Ball x60/x64, Oldottosea x2C and attribute offsets x8-x24, Great Fox Laser x20/x28, Kyasarin fields, and Climbers Ice f32 offsets.

Cleaned up several function declarations from placeholders to typed prototypes: it_802C1854(Item_GObj*), it_8028EB88(Item_GObj*, Vec3*, f32, f32), it_802D4C74(Item_GObj*), it_802D208C(Item_GObj*), it_802B3554(Item_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*), and Ness yoyo helpers.
Adjusted itclimbersice.c to pass the gobj into it_802C1854, matching the new prototype.

Added dependency includes where matched code needed real types/functions, including ft/fighter.h for Ness yoyo, itfreeze.h/camera/random for Oldottosea, itkyasarinegg.h/random for Kyasarin, and itcoll/lb_00B0/runtime for Great Fox Laser.
Red Shell helper it_8028CFE0 gained an explicit f32 temp before multiplying v.x by -facing_dir, a small codegen-oriented cleanup tied to the motion-5 match.
decomp.dev reported matched code rising to 62.63% (+0.08%) and matched data to 38.10%, with the matched functions plus .sdata/.sdata2 improvements.
smart_moves: Scoped the work as an item-only split from #2345, making the PR easier to review and merge while still delivering multiple matches.
Typed only the proven struct offsets needed by matched code, preserving unknown padding elsewhere instead of over-naming speculative fields.
Converted placeholder prototypes to real signatures at the same time as using the functions, reducing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS friction for later matches.

Used existing engine helpers and callbacks directly, such as Item_80268E5C, efLib_PauseAll/ResumeAll, mpCollSetFacingDir, it_80275444, it_80274CAC, and jumped_on callbacks, rather than reimplementing behavior.
Preserved compiler-sensitive details such as PAD_STACK, local temporary variables, external constants, and assignment ordering where needed for 100% matches.
Kept unconfirmed semantic names as offset-style xNN fields while still making the layouts more useful and searchable.
decomp_lessons: Item matches often require header and struct work first: replacing padding with typed fields at exact offsets can unlock code without requiring final semantic names.
For item motion callbacks, Anim functions commonly return bool transition signals while Phys helpers are void; matching code should respect that callback shape.
Use typed prototypes as soon as callsites prove argument lists. This PR shows several successful replacements for UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS across item headers.
Do not inline float/double literals blindly when the binary uses data constants; this PR used extern constants such as it_804DD150, it_804DD358, it_804DD360, it_804DD728, and it_804DD73C.
Matrix-based item link code may need exact PSMTXIdentity, translation-slot writes, HSD_JObjSetupMatrix, and PSMTXConcat sequencing to match.
Small codegen choices matter: PAD_STACK(8), temporary variables, chained zero assignments, and Vec3 component assignment order were part of the successful matches.
Existing xDD4_itemVar union aliases may appear in matched code before all item-specific item vars are semantically cleaned up; avoid forcing speculative names just for readability.
naming_conventions: Matched motion callbacks follow the project pattern it<Item>_UnkMotion<N>_Anim/Phys/Coll, e.g. itHitodeman_UnkMotion1_Anim and itKirbycutterbeam_UnkMotion0_Phys.
Unidentified helper functions retain address-based names such as it_802CA074, it_802E27B4, and it_802E16F8.
Header declarations keep address comments like /* 2BF800 */ beside prototypes.
Item variable and attribute structs use it<Item>_ItemVars or it<Item>Attributes names with offset-style members such as x64, x2C, x14, and pad_2C until semantics are known.

Project item spellings are preserved exactly, including existing lowercase file/module names such as ithitodeman, itoldottosea, itpatapata, itrshell, and itzgshell, and the existing function spelling itZrshell_UnkMotion6_Phys.
Typed gobj parameters use project aliases such as Item_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*, and HSD_GObj* rather than generic void pointers when proven.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) in matched functions where needed for stack layout, including Hitodeman, Kirby cutter beam, and Patapata.
Introduced f32 temp in it_8028CFE0 so v.x *= -facing_dir matched through a separate temporary.
Declared extern data constants instead of literals for matching loads: f64 it_804DD358/it_804DD360 in itkamex and f32 it_804DD150/it_804DD73C/it_804DD728 elsewhere.
Used exact HSD scale APIs and Vec3 locals rather than direct partial writes for JObj scale operations.
Matched yoyo world-position extraction with PSMTXIdentity, m[0][3]/m[1][3]/m[2][3] zeroing via it_804DD150, HSD_JObjSetupMatrix, and PSMTXConcat.
Matched shell slowdown with ABS(ip->x40_vel.x) threshold checks against attrs->x8 and attrs->x30, then repeated velocity zeroing and motion/callback reset calls.
Used a raw f32* specialAttributes pointer for Kirby cutter beam where the matched code only needed attr[3], while using typed attribute structs where offsets were known.

review_feedback.note: Author stated this was split from #2345 and limited to item-related 100% matches.
Reported 13 new matches, +2976 matched code bytes, +40 matched data bytes, and additional .sdata2 improvements.
jurrejelle approved the PR with no review body and there were no inline review comments in the slice.
